tossups.question: In a film by this director, a bomb explodes midway through a bedtime story about three kittens who lost their mittens. French critics praised this director’s film about an orphan who hunts a diamond with the gentleman smuggler Jeremy Fox. This director showed a woman reacting to the discovery of her suicided husband by dialing the phone and coolly stating “this is Tom Duncan’s widow.” MGM made this director cast a white lead for a film in which a mob tries to lynch a mechanic played by Spencer Tracy. (*) Lee Marvin throws boiling coffee in Gloria Grahame’s face in a noir by this director that follows Dave Bannion, a fired cop hunting his wife’s killers. For 10 points, what director of Fury and The Big Heat is better known for German films like M?
tossups.question_sanitized: In a film by this director, a bomb explodes midway through a bedtime story about three kittens who lost their mittens. French critics praised this director's film about an orphan who hunts a diamond with the gentleman smuggler Jeremy Fox. This director showed a woman reacting to the discovery of her suicided husband by dialing the phone and coolly stating "this is Tom Duncan's widow." MGM made this director cast a white lead for a film in which a mob tries to lynch a mechanic played by Spencer Tracy. (*) Lee Marvin throws boiling coffee in Gloria Grahame's face in a noir by this director that follows Dave Bannion, a fired cop hunting his wife's killers. For 10 points, what director of Fury and The Big Heat is better known for German films like M?
tossups.answer: Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang] (The second clue refers to Moonfleet.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang] (The second clue refers to Moonfleet.)

tossups.question: A movie series by this director follows a villainous hypnotist who uses his powers to win at gambling. A character in one film by this director is introduced with an odd crotch perspective and an opening tracking shot in that film from this director shows a washerwoman complaining about a children's rhyme. Another dream sequence in that film shows Freder watching workers march into the mouth of a machine Moloch. One character in a film by this director hums "In the Hall of the Mountain King" before killing his victims. A film by this director shows the scientist Rotwang transferring Maria's consciousness into a robot woman to unleash chaos in the namesake city. For 10 points, name this German expressionist director of M and Metropolis.
tossups.question_sanitized: A movie series by this director follows a villainous hypnotist who uses his powers to win at gambling. A character in one film by this director is introduced with an odd crotch perspective and an opening tracking shot in that film from this director shows a washerwoman complaining about a children's rhyme. Another dream sequence in that film shows Freder watching workers march into the mouth of a machine Moloch. One character in a film by this director hums "In the Hall of the Mountain King" before killing his victims. A film by this director shows the scientist Rotwang transferring Maria's consciousness into a robot woman to unleash chaos in the namesake city. For 10 points, name this German expressionist director of M and Metropolis.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang]

tossups.question: One film by this director opens with a montage of images such as a cash register, a silver bracelet, and a woman with elaborate curlers in her hair while a man sings a song on the theme "You cannot get something for nothing." A woman stumbles through a crowd all staring at a burning building before cutting to the protagonist holding a dog named Rainbow in the lynch mob scene of one of this director's better American films. The (+) rocket scientist Hermann Oberth was a consultant for a film by this director with a screenplay written by his wife Thea that was one of the first semi-plausible takes on a lunar landing. This director of You and Me, (*) Fury, and Woman in the Moon is better known for a film where a character in an abandoned distillery declares, "I can not help myself! I have no control over this evil thing that is inside me—the fire, the voices, the torment!" That film by this director opens with a girl being offered a balloon by a man who whistles "In the Hall of the Mountain King." For 10 points, name this German director of M and Metropolis.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang]
tossups.question_sanitized: One film by this director opens with a montage of images such as a cash register, a silver bracelet, and a woman with elaborate curlers in her hair while a man sings a song on the theme "You cannot get something for nothing." This director of You and Me, (*) Fury, and Woman in the Moon is better known for a film where a character in an abandoned distillery declares, "I can not help myself! I have no control over this evil thing that is inside me-the fire, the voices, the torment!" That film by this director opens with a girl being offered a balloon by a man who whistles "In the Hall of the Mountain King." For 10 points, name this German director of M and Metropolis.

tossups.question: A film by this director ends with a shot of three women in black crying, with one of them speaking a warning to an unseen audience. Gottfried Huppertz used the dies irae motif in the score of one this man's films to accompany rapid cuts between overlapping eyes staring out of the screen, a crowd of frenzied men, and a woman dancing. During his transition to the sound era, this man often collaborated with his wife, screenwriter Thea von Harbou. In one film by this director, a blind vendor identifies a (*) serial killer who murders children, who was later marked with the title letter. In an early science fiction film by this man, the inventor Rotwang transferred the likeliness of Maria onto his robot. For 10 points, name this Weimar-era German director, whose films include M and Metropolis.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang]
tossups.question_sanitized: A film by this director ends with a shot of three women in black crying, with one of them speaking a warning to an unseen audience. Gottfried Huppertz used the dies irae motif in the score of one this man's films to accompany rapid cuts between overlapping eyes staring out of the screen, a crowd of frenzied men, and a woman dancing. In an early science fiction film by this man, the inventor Rotwang transferred the likeliness of Maria onto his robot. For 10 points, name this Weimar-era German director, whose films include M and Metropolis.

tossups.question: In her essay, “Circles and Squares,” Pauline Kael wondered why John Huston’s early works were rejected while this man’s embarrassing “Indian Epic,” released in America as Journey to the Lost City, was praised. One of his films sees Professor Novotny taken hostage in an attempt to get Mascha to reveal the location of a man who has assassinated Reinhard Heydrich. This director of Hangmen Also Die starred as himself directing a film adaptation of the Odyssey in a 1963 film by Jean-Luc Godard. One of his films opens with the title character hypnotizing the hero, Edgar Hull. He told of the “Thousand Eyes” and “Testament” of the title gambler in his sequels to his film about Dr. Mabuse. In another of his films, the whistling of “In the Hall of the Mountain King” helps a blind street vendor identify Hans Beckert as a child murderer. For 10 points, name this German director of M and Metropolis.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang]

In another of his films, the whistling of "In the Hall of the Mountain King" helps a blind street vendor identify Hans Beckert as a child murderer. For 10 points, name this German director of M and Metropolis.

bonuses.leadin: This character breathes through a punctured wine bottle while hiding in a basement cistern near the end of a film titled for his battle of wits with his nemesis Inspector Juve. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this criminal mastermind, the star of five internationally-popular French silent films directed by Louis Feuillade from 1913-14.

A fan club called the Society of the Friends of Fantômas was co-founded by two early proponents of this artistic movement. A razor slices a woman’s eyeball in a seminal film from this movement, Un Chien Andalou.

This director drew from Juve contre Fantômas to create a sequence in which the title character shuffles a deck of cards to choose a disguise to perpetrate a stock market panic, in the opening sequence of his first film about the criminal psychologist Dr. Mabuse.
answers: Surrealism [the Society of the Friends of Fantômas was founded by Guillaume Apollinaire and Max Jacob]
answers_sanitized: Surrealism [the Society of the Friends of Fantomas was founded by Guillaume Apollinaire and Max Jacob]
Fritz Lang [or Friedrich Christian Anton Lang]

bonuses.leadin: A character whistles this theme after his shadow appears on his own wanted poster before he buys a girl a balloon. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this musical piece which accompanies a child murderer who is hunted down by a criminal network in a 1931 film.

"In the Hall of the Mountain King" is whistled by a man who murders to "escape [himself]" in M, a film by this director. This director pioneered the world of science fiction in cinema with his silent film Metropolis.
Fritz Lang belonged to this country's school of "expressionism." It's competitiveness in the worldwide film industry largely fell apart after directors like Lang fled due to the rise of Hitler.
answers: "In the Hall of the Mountain King" [or "I Dovregubbens hall" or "In der Halle des Bergkönigs"; prompt on Peer Gynt]
Federal Republic of Germany [or Bundesrepublik Deutschland; accept Weimar Republic or Weimar Germany]

bonuses.leadin: In 1978, 533 previously lost silent film reels were discovered in this country, as documented in Bill Morrison’s Dawson City: Frozen Time. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this home country of David Cronenberg and Guy Maddin, as well as the francophone directors Xavier Dolan (“gzahv-YAY doh-LAWN”) and Denis Villeneuve.

Another significant moment in the rediscovery of lost silent films was the 2008 find of the lost reels of this 1927 sci-fi film in Buenos Aires. In a famous shot from this film, the likeness of the labor leader Maria is given to a robot.

The Moonshiners, the now-lost first narrative film ever made in this country, was remade in 2017 using only a written synopsis. This country’s most acclaimed director made the films The Match Factory Girl and The Man Without A Past.
answers: Finland [or Republic of Finland] (The director is Aki Kaurismäki.)

packet.name: E - Central Florida A, Illinois A, Penn State B, and UNC B

answers_sanitized: Finland [or Republic of Finland] (The director is Aki Kaurismaki.)

bonuses.leadin: This character creates a stock market panic, seduces Countess Told, and battles Norbert von Wenk before being confined to an insane asylum and writing his will before his spirit comes back. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this villainous hypnotist, who features in a trilogy of German Expressionist films in which he is “a Gambler,” gives “Testament,” and has a “Thousand Eyes.”

A Robert Wiene (“VEE-nuh”) horror film is titled for “The Cabinet of” this director of an insane asylum, who hypnotizes the sleepwalker Cesare into killing people. That film is known for its extreme shadows and angular sets.
Dr. Mabuse was created by this German expressionist director, who is more famous for the film noir M and the science-fiction film Metropolis.
